Given: A user is ready for OTP delivery after entering valid credentials
Then: An OTP is delivered within 10 seconds to the registered contact (SMS/Email)

Given: A user has SMS registered as their OTP delivery method
When: The user completes login credentials entry
Then: An OTP SMS is delivered to the registered phone number within acceptable time

Given: A user has email registered as their OTP delivery method
When: The user completes login credentials entry
Then: An OTP email is delivered to the registered email address within acceptable time

Given: An OTP delivery request is made
When: The SMS/Email service experiences a network failure
Then: The system displays an error message to the user and offers to retry OTP delivery

When: The third-party OTP service is temporarily unavailable
Then: The system displays a user-friendly error message and provides retry option

Given: A user's registered contact information is outdated or incorrect
When: TFA is triggered and OTP delivery is attempted
Then: The system attempts delivery to the invalid address and logs the failure; user should be notified to update contact info

When: The user clicks 'Resend OTP' button multiple times in sequence
Then: A new OTP is generated and sent each time; previous OTPs are invalidated

Given: A user is requesting OTP codes
When: The user rapidly clicks 'Resend OTP' 5+ times within a few seconds
Then: The system implements rate limiting, allows limited retries, and displays guidance or locks further attempts

Given: Multiple OTP requests are made in quick succession
When: The system processes concurrent OTP requests from the same user
Then: Only one unique OTP is generated and sent; duplicate requests are queued or rejected appropriately

Given: Multiple users (100+) are logging in simultaneously
When: OTP requests are triggered for all users
Then: All OTP deliveries are processed without delays or dropped requests; delivery SLA is maintained

Given: The system is experiencing peak login traffic
When: OTP delivery service is processing requests at maximum capacity
Then: System maintains acceptable delivery time (within 10 seconds) without degrading performance
The system logs the successful delivery with timestamp, user ID, and delivery method

When: The delivery service returns an error
Then: The system logs the failed attempt with error reason, timestamp, and retry count for audit purposes

Given: The primary OTP service provider is down
Then: The system automatically failovers to secondary OTP provider and successfully delivers the OTP

When: The OTP is sent to the user
Then: The OTP matches expected format (numeric, correct length 4-6 digits) and is readable

Given: A user is on the OTP entry screen
When: Time passes and user hasn't entered OTP yet
Then: A 'Resend OTP' button is available and user can quickly request another OTP without restarting login

Given: An OTP is about to expire (at 9 minutes 59 seconds)
When: The user submits the OTP for validation
Then: The system accepts the OTP and grants access (assuming standard 10-minute expiry)

Given: An OTP has just expired (past 10 minutes)
When: The user submits the OTP for validation
Then: The system rejects the OTP as expired and prompts user to request a new one
